issues: outline_color_mismatch: peers use dark gray/black outlines; candidate uses warm gold/yellow as primary border — fundamentally different outline idiom
color_temperature_breach: peer sheet dominated by cool stone grays and teal; candidate is warm gold + white + blue accent — palette temperature clash
palette_overlap_low: gold/yellow is entirely absent from peer tileset; only shared hue is a faint cyan echo; overlap score ~15%
highlight_intensity_excessive: candidate interior is near-white, far brighter than any peer tile; peers stay in muted mid-tone range
rendering_style_divergence: candidate reads as a smooth UI chrome element with gradient-like gold graduation; peers are flat gritty pixel tiles with no gradient

charm_level_mismatch: peers are grim dungeon tiles (zero cuteness); candidate is ornate decorative frame — still not 'cute Echo' but in the opposite direction (regal/formal vs gritty/raw), adding tonal discord
shadow_direction_absent: peers show subtle top-left pixel shadows; candidate frame has no directional shadow cue at all
reasoning: Evaluated across all 11 dims. Outline weight is comparable (both fine-pixel scale) and detail density is similarly moderate, giving 2 partial passes. However 6 of 11 dims show clear breaks: the gold warm palette is wholly foreign to the cool stone/teal/red peer family; the near-white interior blows past the muted peer highlight ceiling; the smooth ornate chrome rendering sits in a different visual register from flat dungeon tiles; palette overlap is negligible (~15%); charm level lands in an incompatible tonal space (regal vs grim). The candidate looks like it was pulled from a different game's UI kit rather than the same Denzi sprite sheet. Score 4 — fail.
